metadata.title: MailKit — Email on your domain in 5 minutes
metadata.description: Skip 30 minutes of DNS hell. Automated Cloudflare + Brevo setup with guided Gmail Send-As. $5 per mailbox.

announcementBanner.message: Launch week — first 100 setups free with promo FIRST100

hero.eyebrow: Email infrastructure for indie makers and small teams
hero.subhead: We configure Cloudflare, Brevo, and DKIM/SPF/DMARC records. You copy-paste four lines into Gmail. That's the whole product.
hero.trustNote: 30-day money-back. Auto-refund if our setup fails.

mockup.alt: Gmail Compose window showing your custom domain email as the From address
mockup.subjectPlaceholder: Quick update on the Q2 launch

integrations.label: Built on infrastructure trusted by millions

pricing.heading: Five dollars. One time. Done.
pricing.subhead: No subscription. No recurring fee. No surprise charges next month.
inclusions: Unlimited free aliases on your domain
pricing.paymentNote: Powered by Lemon Squeezy. Stripe, Apple Pay, Google Pay accepted.
pricing.compare: Compare: Google Workspace $6/user/mo. ImprovMX $9/mo. MailKit $5 once.

howItWorks.heading: Three automated steps. One you do yourself.
howItWorks.subheading: Total time under 5 minutes. Total work on your side: four copy-paste actions.

step1.title: Cloudflare Email Routing
step1.body: We enable Email Routing on your domain and write the MX record. You don't see Brevo at all.

step2.title: Brevo SMTP setup
step2.body: We claim your domain in our shared Brevo account and write DKIM and brevo-code records.

step3.title: DNS verification
step3.body: We verify SPF passes, DKIM signs correctly, and DMARC alignment is OK before handing off.

step4.title: Gmail Send-As
step4.body: We hand you four lines: SMTP server, port, login, password. You paste into Gmail Settings.

problem.heading: Setting up email on your own domain is a 90-minute headache.
problem.subhead: Five tabs open. Three documentation portals. One typo in SPF and you start over.

trust.heading: We refund first, ask questions later.
trust.subhead: Two-tier guarantee that does the talking, not the marketing.

automation.title: Automation failure
automation.body: If our automated setup fails on our end (Cloudflare or Brevo phase) — full refund within 24 hours. Automatic. No request needed.

functional.title: 30-day functional
functional.body: If within 30 days you can't actually send email through your domain — even after our support — full refund on request. Just email us.

items.a: MailKit costs $5 once per mailbox. No subscription, no recurring charges. That covers the full automated setup: Cloudflare Email Routing for receiving, Brevo SMTP for sending, and the guided Gmail Send-As wizard. Compared to Google Workspace at $6/user/month ($72/year per mailbox), MailKit is a one-time fee.

items.q: Can I do this myself for free?
items.a: Yes — by hand it takes 60–90 minutes for an experienced engineer and 3+ hours for everyone else, juggling Cloudflare Email Routing, Brevo SMTP, five DNS records (MX, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, brevo-code) and the Gmail Send-As wizard. MailKit collapses that into about 5 minutes for $5: ~15 sec for the Cloudflare phase, ~60 sec for Brevo, ~3 min of copy-paste in Gmail.

items.q: How is this different from Google Workspace?
items.a: Google Workspace gives you a separate inbox at $6/user/month forever — a new mailbox to check, a separate password, full migration if you stop paying. MailKit configures your existing Gmail to send and receive from your domain via Send-As. Same Gmail inbox, no migration, no monthly fee. The trade-off: with MailKit your domain email lives inside your personal Gmail, not in a Workspace tenant.

items.q: Does it work with my domain registered at Namecheap, GoDaddy, or other registrars?
items.a: Yes. The only requirement is that the domain's DNS is on Cloudflare. If it isn't yet, the one-time step is moving the nameservers to Cloudflare (free, takes 5 minutes at your registrar's panel) — Cloudflare's docs walk you through it. After that, MailKit handles the rest through the Cloudflare API.

items.q: What if I already use ImprovMX or another forwarding service?
items.a: MailKit will replace ImprovMX with Cloudflare Email Routing. You'll need to remove the existing MX records on your domain before setup; our wizard detects conflicts and tells you exactly which records block the run. Once switched, you'll have both receiving (Cloudflare) and outbound sending (Brevo SMTP via Gmail) — ImprovMX free tier doesn't do outbound.

items.q: Why do I have to do the Gmail step manually instead of you fully automating it?
items.a: The Gmail API method that adds Send-As entries (gmail.settings.sendAs.create) is blocked on personal @gmail.com accounts — it requires Google Workspace with domain-wide delegation. We made that part a 3-minute guided wizard with copy-paste values instead. The other technical parts (Cloudflare + Brevo + DNS) are fully automated.

If our Cloudflare or Brevo automation fails, we issue an automatic full refund within 24 hours. The dashboard shows you which step failed and why. If you'd like to retry once we've fixed our side, we'll let you know — no extra charge if we fixed something on our end.

items.q: Why might my emails go to spam?
items.a: MailKit configures technically correct SPF/DKIM/DMARC authentication. Whether email lands in a specific recipient's inbox depends on sender reputation, content, sending practices, and the recipient's policies — all outside MailKit's control. Domain warmup, recipient list hygiene, and consent compliance are the sender's job. Practical advice: send 10–20 emails per day in the first week from a fresh domain, then ramp up gradually.

items.q: Is my Cloudflare API token safe with you?
items.a: We use the token only during the active setup session and discard it from memory afterwards. We do not persist it in our database. The token can be scoped to the minimum permissions our wizard documents (Zone:DNS:Edit + Email Routing edits on the specific zone) and can be revoked from your Cloudflare dashboard at any time.

items.q: How do I get a refund if I want one?
items.a: Two paths. (1) If automation failed on our end, the refund is automatic within 24 hours — you don't have to ask. (2) For a 30-day functional refund, email support@getmailkit.com explaining what's not working. We respond within 1 business day and refund within 7 days if support can't resolve it.

finalCta.heading: Five minutes from now, you'll have email on your domain.
finalCta.subhead: Or your money back, automatically. No tickets, no calls, no questions.

dashboard.title: Welcome back, {name}
dashboard.subtitle: Your domain setups, purchases, and account at a glance.

emptyState.title: Set up your first domain
emptyState.body: You haven't started a setup yet. It takes about 5 minutes — paste a Cloudflare token, pick your zone, follow the Gmail wizard.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete your MailKit account?
deleteConfirm.body: This removes your profile, setup runs, and purchase records from MailKit. Your domain's DNS records and Gmail Send-As config stay where they are — those live in Cloudflare and Gmail. This can't be undone.

setup.title: Connect your domain
setup.subtitle: Three steps: paste your Cloudflare token, pick the zone, watch us wire it up.

step1.title: Step 1 — Paste your Cloudflare API token
step1.tokenHelp: Create at https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens — template Custom token, scopes: Zone:Zone:Read, Zone:DNS:Edit, Zone:Zone Settings:Edit, Zone:Email Routing Rules:Edit (all four zone-scoped; set Zone Resource to the specific zone) + Account:Email Routing Addresses:Edit (account-scoped).

step2.title: Step 2 — Pick the zone and mailbox
step2.mailboxHint: This becomes {local}@your-domain — lowercase letters, digits, dot, dash, underscore.

awaitingVerify.title: Check your Gmail inbox
awaitingVerify.body: Cloudflare sent a verification link to {email}. Click it, then retry.

done.title: Cloudflare is ready
done.body: Your domain now routes {mailbox}@{domain} to Gmail. Next up: Brevo SMTP — coming in Ticket #4b.

failed.title: Setup failed
failed.body: We couldn't finish the Cloudflare step. Fix the issue below and restart from Step 1.
failed.bodyBrevo: We couldn't finish the Brevo step. Fix the issue below and restart from Step 1.

errors.invalid_token: The API token is invalid or missing required permissions.
errors.zone_not_found: Zone not found in your Cloudflare account.
errors.dns_rejected: Cloudflare rejected a DNS record. Details: {details}
errors.rate_limited: Too many requests. Try again in a minute.
errors.cloudflare_unavailable: Cloudflare is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.no_primary_email: Your Google account email is missing — sign out and sign back in.
errors.run_wrong_state: This setup run is not in the right state for this action.
errors.run_corrupt: Setup run is missing required data; start a new one.
errors.db_insert_failed: Could not record the setup run. Try again.
errors.brevo_invalid_token: Brevo rejected our API key. Hold tight while we rotate it — nothing for you to do.
errors.brevo_domain_taken: Brevo already knows this domain from a previous run — we picked up the existing setup.
errors.brevo_rate_limited: Brevo throttled our requests. Retrying shortly.
errors.brevo_unavailable: Brevo is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.brevo_verify_timeout: Brevo could not verify your domain yet. DNS propagation can take a few minutes — retry soon.
errors.brevo_state_unrecoverable: Brevo knows this domain but did not return the DKIM records we need to configure DNS. The existing Brevo entry is in a partial state — please ping support.
errors.spf_conflict: Your SPF record ends in -all (strict policy). Soften it to ~all so Brevo can send on your behalf, then retry.
errors.dns_write_failed: We could not write a DNS record cleanly. Details: {details}
errors.brevo_smtp_misconfigured: Our SMTP relay is not configured yet. This is on us — nothing to fix on your side; ping support if you see this for more than a minute.

brevoSteps.dns: Brevo DNS records written (DKIM, brevo-code, SPF merged, DMARC)

recheck.title: Waiting for Brevo to verify DNS
recheck.body: DNS records are in place. Brevo hasn't confirmed them yet — propagation usually takes a few minutes.

terminal.title: Email infrastructure ready
terminal.body: Brevo now handles outbound mail for {mailbox}@{domain}. One step left — Gmail Send-As.

gmailStep.title: Gmail Send-As — coming soon
gmailStep.body: This step is Ticket #6. Your Cloudflare routing and Brevo SMTP are already live — you'll plug Gmail in as the last link once the UI lands.

intro.title: Last step — Gmail Send-As
intro.subtitle: About 3 minutes of copy-paste. You'll add {target} to Gmail so you can send from it like a regular address.

openSettings.title: Open Gmail → Settings
openSettings.body: In Gmail, click the ⚙ gear (top-right) → See all settings → Accounts and Import → Send mail as → Add another email address.

senderInfo.title: Fill in sender info
senderInfo.body: Paste these into the first Gmail dialog. Leave "Treat as an alias" checked.

smtpSettings.title: SMTP settings
smtpSettings.body: Gmail asks for an SMTP server next. Paste these four values. The password is your only chance to copy it — keep it safe.
smtpSettings.passwordWarning: We don't store this password for you. Copy it now; if you lose it, ping support and we'll help.

verificationEmail.title: Gmail will email a confirmation code
verificationEmail.body: Gmail sends a 9-digit code to {target}. Because Cloudflare forwards {target} to your inbox, it will arrive in the same Gmail you're using. Open the email, click the link OR paste the code back into the Gmail dialog.

confirm.title: Confirm it works
confirm.body: Open Gmail → Compose → the From field should now let you pick {target}. Send a quick test email to yourself to be sure.
confirm.checkbox: I added the address and verified it in Gmail

done.title: Done — you're sending from your domain
done.body: Compose in Gmail, switch From to {target}, send. Receipts land in your inbox as usual.
done.warmupTip: Tip: warm up the domain gradually — 10–20 emails per day in the first week, then ramp up. Bursts from a fresh domain are the fastest way to land in spam.

errors.copyFailed: Your browser blocked clipboard access. Select the value and copy it by hand.
errors.checkboxRequired: Please confirm you've added and verified the address in Gmail.

footer.tagline: Email on your domain. In 5 minutes. Guaranteed.
footer.copyright: © 2026 MailKit. Built by an indie hacker.

error.title: Something broke on our end
error.body: An unexpected error happened. We've logged it and we're looking into it. You can try refreshing the page or heading back home.

cookieConsent.body: We use only the cookies needed to sign you in via Google and remember your locale. No marketing trackers, no ad pixels.
